document.name: coding-redesign-cli-showcase
document.title: Coding redesign (CLI): clone repo → plan/generate/critic → PR (Playwright-MCP critic)
document.summary: The plan agent clones PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo, then a plan/generate/critic loop redesigns the SvelteKit landing page: the CRITIC drives the official Playwright MCP server (in-pod, stdio) to open the running app in a real Chromium, take an accessibility snapshot + screenshot, and judge it against a 4-dimension design rubric. A deterministic build gate must pass, the critic writes a machine-readable verdict.json the loop reads, and the result is opened as a pull request. GITHUB_TOKEN auto-injected; interactive-cli workspace-backend family.

intent.title: Redesign request
intent.default: Redesign the Nimbus Notes landing page (src/routes/+page.svelte plus a global stylesheet) into a polished, DISTINCTIVE product landing page with a coherent visual identity — strong typography hierarchy, deliberate color/spacing, a hero, the three feature cards, a pricing section, and a clear primary CTA. Plain SvelteKit + CSS only; keep `npm run build` passing; do NOT change package.json deps or routing.
intent.description: What the agents change in the repo.

planAgent.title: Planner agent
planAgent.description: PLAN phase agent (clones + writes SPEC). Same workspace-backend family.

intent.title: Redesign request
intent.default: Redesign the Nimbus Notes landing page (src/routes/+page.svelte plus a global stylesheet) into a polished, DISTINCTIVE product landing page with a coherent visual identity — strong typography hierarchy, deliberate color/spacing, a hero, the three feature cards, a pricing section, and a clear primary CTA. Plain SvelteKit + CSS only; keep `npm run build` passing; do NOT change package.json deps or routing.
intent.description: What the agents change in the repo.

planAgent.title: Planner agent
planAgent.description: PLAN phase agent (clones + writes SPEC). Same workspace-backend family.

agentConfig.name: Planner
agentConfig.instructions:
You are the Planner for a coding redesign task on a SvelteKit repo. FIRST clone the repo into /sandbox/work/repo by running EXACTLY this shell command:
  cd /sandbox/work && rm -rf repo && git clone https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git repo && cd repo && git config user.name agent && git config user.email agent@workflow-builder.local && git remote set-url origin https://github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git
Then READ the repo (especially src/routes/+page.svelte and package.json) and write a concise product+design spec to /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (ONLY that file; do NOT edit the repo). SPEC.md needs markdown sections: Objective (one paragraph), Acceptance Criteria (bulleted, checkable — specific sections/components, build passes, no dep changes), and a Design Rubric naming the FOUR dimensions: design_quality (coherent whole, distinct mood/identity), originality (deliberate custom choices vs generic/template defaults), craft (typography hierarchy, spacing, color harmony, contrast), functionality (usable, responsive, accessible). End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Redesign request:
" + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the landing page") + "

Repo to redesign: https://github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo
FIRST clone it into /sandbox/work/repo, authenticating with the GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable, by running this shell:
  cd /sandbox/work && rm -rf repo && git clone https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git repo && cd repo && git config user.email agent@workflow-builder.local && git config user.name agent && git remote set-url origin https://github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git
Then READ the repo (src/routes/+page.svelte, package.json) and write a concise product+design spec to /sandbox/work/SPEC.md with sections Objective, Acceptance Criteria, and a Design Rubric naming the four dimensions design_quality, originality, craft, functionality. Do NOT edit the repo. End with a one-line summary." }

refine.while: ${ ( ( ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS")) ) and ( (( (((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) as $v | ( ($v.meets_criteria) // (($v.overall_verdict // $v.verdict // "") | ascii_downcase | test("pass|approv|meet|satisf|accept")) // (($v.score // 0) >= 80) ) )) == true ) ) | not }

agentConfig.name: Generator
agentConfig.instructions: You are the Generator. Redesign the SvelteKit landing page in /sandbox/work/repo to satisfy /sandbox/work/SPEC.md. FIRST read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md. Edit src/routes/+page.svelte and add a global stylesheet (e.g. src/routes/+layout.svelte importing app.css, or a <style> block) — plain SvelteKit + CSS, NO new dependencies, do NOT touch package.json or routing. You do NOT grade your own work: a deterministic build gate plus an independent design critic (who VIEWS the rendered page) evaluate each attempt. Each turn: address the build output AND critic feedback, make the most impactful improvement, ensure `npm run build` passes, end with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Redesign request: " + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the landing page") + "

Repo: /sandbox/work/repo; spec: /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (follow your instructions)." + "

Your previous attempt:
" + (.loop.last.generate.content // "(first attempt)") + "

Build gate (must end OBJECTIVE PASS):
" + ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") // "(not run yet)") + "

Design critic feedback (from the browser):
" + (try ((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.feedback // .summary // "(none)")) catch "(none)") + "

Make the build pass AND satisfy the critic, then end with a brief summary." }

with.command: cd /sandbox/work/repo && (npm install --no-audit --no-fund >/tmp/install.log 2>&1 && git checkout -- package-lock.json 2>/dev/null; true && npm run build >/tmp/build.log 2>&1 && echo 'OBJECTIVE PASS: build OK' || { echo 'OBJECTIVE FAIL: build failed'; tail -40 /tmp/build.log; }) 2>&1; echo "__EXIT__$?"

set -eu
cd /sandbox/work/repo
PORT=4173
PID_FILE=/sandbox/work/preview.pid
URL_FILE=/sandbox/work/preview.url
LOG_FILE=/sandbox/work/preview.log
SHOT_FILE=/sandbox/work/critic-shot.png
stop_existing() {
  if [ -s "$PID_FILE" ]; then
    old_pid="$(cat "$PID_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [ -n "$old_pid" ] && kill -0 "$old_pid" 2>/dev/null; then
      kill "$old_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
      wait "$old_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
  fi
}
start_server() {
  label="$1"; shift
  echo "starting $label on port $PORT" >>"$LOG_FILE"
  ( "$@" >>"$LOG_FILE" 2>&1 ) &
  echo $! >"$PID_FILE"
}
wait_ready() {
  for i in $(seq 1 60); do
    if curl -sf "http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/" >"$URL_FILE"
      return 0
    fi
    pid="$(cat "$PID_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [ -n "$pid" ] && ! kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
      return 1
    fi
    sleep 1
  done
  return 1
}
stop_existing
rm -f "$PID_FILE" "$URL_FILE" "$SHOT_FILE"
: >"$LOG_FILE"
start_server preview npx vite preview --host 127.0.0.1 --port "$PORT"
if ! wait_ready; then
  stop_existing
  : >"$LOG_FILE"
  start_server dev npx vite dev --host 127.0.0.1 --port "$PORT"
  wait_ready
fi
cat "$URL_FILE"
tail -20 "$LOG_FILE"

agentConfig.name: Critic
agentConfig.instructions:
You are an exacting design Critic with strong taste, independent of the builder; you do NOT edit the repo. Your job is to VIEW the running redesigned app in a real browser and judge it.

STEP 1 - use the workflow-owned preview lifecycle. The start_preview task already started exactly one Vite server and wrote its URL to /sandbox/work/preview.url, PID to /sandbox/work/preview.pid, and logs to /sandbox/work/preview.log. Read the URL from /sandbox/work/preview.url and use that URL. Do not run timeout, pkill, custom test-vite/test-preview scripts, or additional preview/dev commands unless /sandbox/work/preview.url is missing or the URL does not answer.

STEP 2 - inspect with the Playwright MCP browser tools (MCP server name 'playwright'): call browser_navigate to the URL from /sandbox/work/preview.url, then browser_snapshot to read the accessibility tree, and browser_take_screenshot with filename 'critic-shot.png' (saved to /sandbox/work/critic-shot.png) for the visual record. You MAY resize the viewport (desktop/tablet/mobile) and browser_click the nav / primary CTA, re-snapshotting to test the functionality and responsive dimensions. Use the BROWSER (Playwright MCP) for inspection; do not write your own screenshot scripts.

IMPORTANT - build artifacts are EXPECTED and are NOT out-of-scope changes: IGNORE any churn in package-lock.json, node_modules/, .svelte-kit/, and build/ (these come from npm install/npm run build, not from the redesign). Judge ONLY the redesign source (src/) and the rendered UI; do NOT fail the verdict for lockfile or build-output changes.

STEP 3 - read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md and the source (src/routes/+page.svelte, +layout.svelte, any CSS).

STEP 4 - judge against SPEC.md and the FOUR dimensions: design_quality (coherent whole, distinct mood/identity), originality (deliberate custom choices vs generic/template defaults), craft (typography hierarchy, spacing, color harmony, contrast), functionality (usable, responsive, accessible, interactive elements actually work). CALIBRATION: pass ONLY for genuinely polished, distinctive, usable work as SEEN IN THE BROWSER; generic or merely 'fine' is NOT a pass. Cite concrete evidence from what you actually saw on screen.

STEP 5 - WRITE your verdict to the file /sandbox/work/verdict.json (use your file-write tool). It MUST be valid JSON and MUST include a top-level boolean key "meets_criteria" (this boolean is AUTHORITATIVE - set it to true when the redesign genuinely satisfies the rubric) and a numeric "score" on a 0-100 scale (true only if EVERY dimension is clearly strong). You may ALSO include "overall_verdict", "feedback", and richer per-dimension analysis. The verdict.json file is the ONLY thing the loop reads. End with a one-line summary; the workflow will stop the preview server by PID after reading the verdict.

body.prompt: ${ "Judge the redesigned landing page in /sandbox/work/repo against /sandbox/work/SPEC.md by VIEWING the already-running preview URL from /sandbox/work/preview.url with the Playwright MCP tools, then WRITE /sandbox/work/verdict.json. Follow your instructions exactly." }

with.command:
for i in $(seq 1 40); do [ -s /sandbox/work/verdict.json ] && break; sleep 1; done; [ -f /sandbox/work/critic-shot.png ] || cp "$(ls -1 /sandbox/work/shots/*.png 2>/dev/null | head -1)" /sandbox/work/critic-shot.png 2>/dev/null; python3 - <<'PYZZ'
import json,re
try:
    d=json.load(open("/sandbox/work/verdict.json"))
except Exception:
    print(json.dumps({"meets_criteria":False,"feedback":"verdict.json missing/invalid"}));raise SystemExit
mc=d.get("meets_criteria")
txt=(str(d.get("overall_verdict") or d.get("verdict") or "")+" "+str(d.get("summary") or "")).lower()
failw=bool(re.search(r"\b(fail|fails|failed|does not pass|doesn.t pass|not pass|reject\w*|out-of-scope|out of scope)\b",txt))
passw=bool(re.search(r"\b(pass|passes|passed|approved?|meets|satisf\w*|accept\w*)\b",txt))
sc=d.get("score")
scpass=isinstance(sc,(int,float)) and ((sc>=80) or (sc<=10 and sc>=8))
if mc is not None: res=bool(mc)
elif failw: res=False
elif passw: res=True
else: res=scpass
print(json.dumps({"meets_criteria":res,"score":sc,"feedback":(d.get("feedback") or d.get("summary") or "")[:300]}))
PYZZ
if [ -s /sandbox/work/preview.pid ]; then pid="$(cat /sandbox/work/preview.pid 2>/dev/null || true)"; if [ -n "$pid" ]; then kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true; wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true; fi; fi

set -eu
SHOT=/sandbox/work/critic-shot.png
PID_FILE=/sandbox/work/preview.pid
URL_FILE=/sandbox/work/preview.url
LOG_FILE=/sandbox/work/preview.log
PORT=4321
stop_server() {
  if [ -s "$PID_FILE" ]; then
    pid="$(cat "$PID_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [ -n "$pid" ] && kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
      kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
      wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
  fi
}
trap stop_server EXIT
if [ -s "$SHOT" ]; then
  ls -la "$SHOT"
  exit 0
fi
cd /sandbox/work/repo
rm -f "$PID_FILE" "$URL_FILE"
: >"$LOG_FILE"
start_server() {
  label="$1"; shift
  echo "starting $label on port $PORT" >>"$LOG_FILE"
  ( "$@" >>"$LOG_FILE" 2>&1 ) &
  echo $! >"$PID_FILE"
}
wait_ready() {
  for i in $(seq 1 60); do
    if curl -sf "http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/" >"$URL_FILE"
      return 0
    fi
    pid="$(cat "$PID_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [ -n "$pid" ] && ! kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
      return 1
    fi
    sleep 1
  done
  return 1
}
start_server preview npx vite preview --host 127.0.0.1 --port "$PORT"
if ! wait_ready; then
  stop_server
  : >"$LOG_FILE"
  start_server dev npx vite dev --host 127.0.0.1 --port "$PORT"
  wait_ready
fi
URL="$(cat "$URL_FILE")"
CHROME="$(find /opt/pw-browsers -path '*/chrome-linux64/chrome' -type f | head -1 || true)"
if [ -z "$CHROME" ]; then CHROME="$(command -v chromium || command -v chromium-browser || command -v google-chrome || true)"; fi
if [ -z "$CHROME" ]; then echo NO_CHROME; exit 1; fi
"$CHROME" --headless --no-sandbox --disable-gpu --disable-dev-shm-usage --window-size=1280,900 --screenshot="$SHOT" "$URL" >>"$LOG_FILE" 2>&1
test -s "$SHOT"
ls -la "$SHOT"

artifacts.title: Rendered redesign screenshot
artifacts.description: Screenshot captured from the final rendered app and persisted as a workflow artifact.

with.command: cd /sandbox/work/repo && git config user.email agent@workflow-builder.local && git config user.name 'workflow-builder agent' && BR=redesign-$(date +%s) && git checkout -b $BR && git add -A && (git commit -m 'Redesign Nimbus Notes landing page (workflow-builder plan/generate/critic)' || echo nothing-to-commit) && git push https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git HEAD:$BR && PR=$(curl -sS -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" -H 'Accept: application/vnd.github+json' https://api.github.com/repos/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo/pulls -d "{\"title\":\"Redesign landing page (automated)\",\"head\":\"$BR\",\"base\":\"main\",\"body\":\"Automated redesign via the workflow-builder plan/generate/critic loop; a Playwright critic inspected the rendered UI.\"}") && echo "$PR" | python3 -c 'import sys,json;d=json.load(sys.stdin);print("PR_URL="+str(d.get("html_url") or d.get("message") or d))'

set.terminalState: ${ if ( ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS")) and ((( (((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) as $v | ( ($v.meets_criteria) // (($v.overall_verdict // $v.verdict // "") | ascii_downcase | test("pass|approv|meet|satisf|accept")) // (($v.score // 0) >= 80) ) )) == true) ) then "satisfied" else "max_iterations_reached" end }

artifacts.title: Coding redesign result
artifacts.description: Build + critic verdicts, iterations, PR output.
